Failing test: "should oscillate a vertical blinker into a horizontal blinker"
Expected: nextGeneration([[0,0],[0,1],[0,2]]) returns [[-1,1],[0,1],[1,1]] (any order)
Current error: only [[0,1]] returned - dead-cell reproduction not implemented
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_18-41-51_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts

Need to add reproduction rule: dead cells with exactly 3 live neighbors become alive. Consider iterating over candidate cells = union of live cells and their neighbors.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass.
I'll implement the minimal change to handle dead cell reproduction by iterating over candidate cells (live cells + their neighbors).

content.text:
All 5 active tests pass (2 remain as todos).

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added candidate-cell iteration over live cells + their neighbors, combined with separate survival rule (alive with 2 or 3 neighbors) and reproduction rule (dead with exactly 3 neighbors).
- **Result**: All tests now pass (5 passing, 2 todo)
- **Approach**: Minimal expansion of the existing logic. Introduced an `isAlive` helper and a candidate set (live cells plus their 8 neighbors via a deduping Set) so dead cells can become alive. The blinker case now produces `[-1,1], [0,1], [1,1]` as expected. No future features added.

Relevant file:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_18-41-51_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
